description.description: A brief description of the series content.

imageUrl.description: URL of the series cover image.

seriesId.description: Reference to the Series this lecture belongs to. (Relationship: Series 1:N Lecture)

description.description: A brief description of the lecture content.

audioUrl.description: URL of the audio file for the lecture.

youtubeUrl.description: URL of the YouTube video for the lecture, if available.

transcriptUrl.description: URL of the transcript (PDF) for the lecture, if available.

duration.description: The duration of the lecture in minutes.

pdfUrl.description: URL of the PDF file for the book.

imageUrl.description: URL of the book cover image.

QuestionAnswer.title: QuestionAnswer
QuestionAnswer.description: Represents a question and its answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the question and answer.

ScheduledLesson.title: ScheduledLesson
ScheduledLesson.description: Represents a scheduled lesson or live stream event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the scheduled lesson.

title.description: The title of the scheduled lesson.

dateTime.description: The date and time of the scheduled lesson.

isLive.description: Indicates whether the lesson is a live stream.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a user comment on a lecture.

lectureId.description: Reference to the Lecture this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Lecture 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N Comment)

text.description: The text content of the comment.

timestamp.description: The date and time the comment was created.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

email.description: The email address of the user.

favoriteLectureIds.description: References to the user's favorite lectures.

LectureRating.title: LectureRating
LectureRating.description: Represents a user's rating for a lecture.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lecture rating.

lectureId.description: Reference to the Lecture being rated. (Relationship: Lecture 1:N LectureRating)

userId.description: Reference to the User who is rating the lecture. (Relationship: User 1:N LectureRating)

rating.description: The rating given by the user (e.g., 1 to 5).

definition.description: Stores series data. Public read access, admin-restricted write access.

params.name: seriesId
params.description: The unique identifier for the series.

definition.description: Stores lecture data. Public read access, admin-restricted write access.

params.name: lectureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lecture.

definition.description: Stores book data. Public read access, admin-restricted write access.

params.name: bookId
params.description: The unique identifier for the book.

definition.description: Stores question and answer data. Public read access, admin-restricted write access.

params.description: The unique identifier for the question and answer.

definition.description: Stores scheduled lesson data. Public read access, admin-restricted write access.

params.name: userId

definition.description: Stores user's favorite lectures. Enables QAPs by allowing listing of favorites under the user's path. Uses path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: lectureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lecture marked as favorite.

definition.description: Stores user's ratings for lectures. Uses path-based ownership.

params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: lectureRatingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lecture rating.

definition.description: Stores comments for each lecture.  This design uses the proper path based ownership of `/lectures/{lectureId}/comments/{commentId}`. Read access can be public, write access requires authentication.

params.name: lectureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lecture.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the comment.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and support secure list operations (QAPs).  Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For instance, if `Comment` access depended on `Lecture` attributes, those attributes would be copied into each `Comment` document.

Structural Segregation is used extensively to ensure each collection has a homogeneous security posture.  For instance, series, lectures, books, Q&A are stored in separate collections.

Access Modeling: User-owned data (favorites, comments, ratings) are placed under `/users/{userId}`. Collaborative data is not present, but would leverage the Membership Map pattern if it existed.

*   `/series`: Stores series information. Publicly accessible for reading, but write access restricted to admins.
*   `/lectures`: Stores lecture information. Publicly accessible for reading, write access restricted to admins.
*   `/books`: Stores book information. Publicly accessible for reading, write access restricted to admins.
*   `/question_answers`: Stores question and answer pairs. Publicly accessible for reading, write access restricted to admins.
*   `/scheduled_lessons`: Stores scheduled lessons. Publicly accessible for reading, write access restricted to admins.
*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores individual user data.
*   `/users/{userId}/favorite_lectures/{lectureId}`: Stores user's favorite lectures. Enables QAPs by allowing listing of favorites under the user's path.  Uses path-based ownership.
*   `/users/{userId}/lecture_ratings/{lectureRatingId}`: Stores user's ratings for lectures.  Uses path-based ownership.
*   `/lectures/{lectureId}/comments/{commentId}`: Stores comments for each lecture. This design uses the proper path based ownership of `/lectures/{lectureId}/comments/{commentId}`. Read access can be public, write access requires authentication.
